content:
In the historic marble quarries of Tuscany, Robotor is revolutionizing sculpture by combining industrial robotics with centuries-old artistry. Founded by Giacomo Massari and Filippo Tincolini, the company uses 13-foot robotic arms to carve marble with unprecedented speed and precision.

• Robotor’s diamond-tipped robotic arms carve marble 10x faster than traditional hand-carving, reducing production from months to days  
• The robots operate autonomously 24/7 using proprietary OR-OS software that converts 3D models into precise toolpaths  
• Clients include major artists like Jeff Koons, Maurizio Cattelan, and Zaha Hadid, as well as museums and corporations worldwide  
• The company aims to carve the largest robot-sculpted statue ever from a 200,000-pound marble block, merging robotics with 500 years of artistic heritage
